summary: Created a brownfield architecture artifact for the admin data-ingestion MVP, constrained to student indicator initial-load batches, explicit preview/apply endpoints, full-store snapshot reuse, and a separate execution-audit store that survives rollback.

decisions.title: Scope Boundary
decisions.decision: Constrain MVP ingestion to student indicator initial-load batches only.
decisions.details: Approved domain write targets are measurements.json and checkpoints.json; execution audit is persisted separately in ingestion_executions.json outside the registered snapshot set. No generic repository-wide JSON writes are allowed.

decisions.title: Admin Entry
decisions.decision: Keep the UI entry inside the existing /app/admin surface.
decisions.details: Use a dedicated admin modal or focused panel launched from AdminPage instead of introducing a new top-level route.

decisions.title: Source Model
decisions.decision: Use an uploaded structured JSON file as the official MVP source.
decisions.details: Frontend reads the file and sends source metadata plus raw_payload as unknown; backend owns authoritative normalization and validation.

decisions.title: API Contract
decisions.decision: Introduce explicit preview/apply endpoints plus execution detail and rollback endpoints.
decisions.details: The architecture defines POST /admin/ingestao-dados/preview, POST /admin/ingestao-dados/apply, GET /admin/ingestao-dados/execucoes/{execution_id}, and POST /admin/ingestao-dados/execucoes/{execution_id}/rollback under the existing admin auth guard.

decisions.title: Validation and Apply Semantics
decisions.decision: Preview and apply share the same validation pipeline, and apply is all-or-nothing for MVP.
decisions.details: The backend validates schema version, batch uniqueness, active student enrollment, product ownership, metric ownership through metric -> protocol -> organization, and numeric/checkpoint rules before any write.

decisions.title: Persistence Rule
decisions.details: Each valid student load replaces measurements and checkpoints for that active enrollment; there is no merge or append mode in MVP.

decisions.title: Backup and Rollback
decisions.decision: Reuse the existing full registered-store snapshot utilities for apply and rollback.
decisions.details: Apply creates a backup snapshot before writes, rollback restores via existing storage_maintenance helpers, and UI only shows safe backup references rather than filesystem paths.

risks.risk: Metric ownership may be under-validated if implementation only checks metric existence.
risks.impact: Indicators could be applied under the wrong product context.
risks.mitigation: Enforce product ownership through metric -> protocol -> organization during preview and apply.

risks.risk: If the execution audit store is added to the registered store catalog, rollback could erase its own evidence.
risks.impact: Operational traceability would be lost after restore.

risks.risk: Partial apply would interact badly with replace-for-enrollment persistence.
risks.impact: Mixed enrollment state would complicate rollback and support.
risks.mitigation: Keep MVP apply all-or-nothing after successful revalidation.

risks.risk: Operational restore may be difficult without a clear runbook.
risks.impact: Backup and execution ids may exist but still be hard to use under pressure.
risks.mitigation: Add a dedicated operational runbook and return execution id plus backup reference in final reports.
